mathml_representations_list: <mstyle id="custom-group-0" class="custom-group"><mi id="mi_167">∂</mi><msub id="msub_24"><mrow><mi id="mi_168">Ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mi id="mi_169">N</mi></mrow></msub></mstyle>

<mstyle id="custom-group-3" class="custom-group"><msup id="msup_7"><mrow><mi id="mi_145">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mrow><mo class="bigl" fence="true">(</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="script" id="mi_146">T</mi><mo>;</mo><mstyle id="custom-group-2" class="custom-group"><msubsup id="msubsup_4"><mrow><mi id="mi_147">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msubsup><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi id="mi_148">Ω</mi><mo>)</mo></mrow></mstyle></mrow><mo class="bigr" fence="true">)</mo></mrow></mstyle>

concepts_list.name: u_field
concepts_list.description: The primary unknown field representing the displacement in the wave equation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'u' appears repeatedly as the dependent variable in the wave equation and in the weak formulation, e.g., 'u(t, x)', 'u in H_0^1(Ω)', and in the weak form as the solution to be found.

concepts_list.name: w_test_function
concepts_list.description: A test function used in the weak formulation to enforce the variational form of the wave equation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'w' is consistently used as a test function in the weak formulation, appearing in integrals with 'u' and in the condition 'for all w in H_0^1(Ω)'.

concepts_list.name: Omega_domain
concepts_list.description: The main spatial computational domain over which the wave equation is defined.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'Ω' is repeatedly used to denote the spatial domain, e.g., in '∫_Ω', 'H_0^1(Ω)', and '∂Ω'. It is the primary spatial domain in the problem.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_interface
concepts_list.description: The collection of interfaces between subdomains where transmission conditions are applied.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'Γ' is explicitly defined as 'the collection of interfaces between the subdomains Ω_i', and appears in integrals over 'Γ' in the weak form.

concepts_list.name: t_time
concepts_list.description: The temporal variable representing time in the evolution of the wave equation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 't' is used in expressions such as 'u(t, x)', '∂²u/∂t²', and 'for a.e. t', indicating its role as the time variable.

concepts_list.name: rho_mass_density
concepts_list.description: The positive mass per unit volume of the medium, a material property in the wave equation.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'ρ' is explicitly defined as 'the positive mass per unit volume' in the text.

concepts_list.name: T_tensile_stress
concepts_list.description: The positive tensile pre-stress applied as a load per unit surface area.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'T' is defined as 'the positive tensile pre-stress as load per unit surface area' in the text.

concepts_list.name: g_boundary_load
concepts_list.description: The boundary load applied on Neumann boundaries, appearing in the weak form as a source term.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'g' appears in the weak form as 'g w dΓ' and is described in the context of boundary conditions on ∂Ω_N.

concepts_list.name: H_0_1_Omega
concepts_list.description: The Sobolev space of functions with zero trace on the boundary, used for the solution and test functions.
concepts_list.justification: The expression 'H_0^1(Ω)' is explicitly defined as the space for both the solution 'u' and test functions 'w', and is referenced multiple times in the weak formulation.

concepts_list.name: H_1_time_dependent
concepts_list.description: The space of functions in H^1(Ω) valued over time, used to describe the regularity of the solution in time.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'H^1(T; H_0^1(Ω))' is introduced as the space of functions satisfying smoothness in time and spatial regularity, and is used to define the solution space for u.

concepts_list.name: H_minus1_dual
concepts_list.description: The dual space of H^{-1}(Ω), used in the duality pairing between the second time derivative of u and the test function w.
concepts_list.justification: The expression 'H^{-1}(Ω)' appears in the weak form as the dual space for the time derivative term, and is explicitly referenced in the duality pairing notation.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: The differential element for integration over the spatial domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'dΩ' appears in integrals such as '∫_Ω ρ ∂²u/∂t² w dΩ', indicating it is the integration variable over the domain Ω.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: The differential element for integration over the interface boundary Γ.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'dΓ' appears in integrals over the interface, such as '∫_Γ [T ∇u] w dΓ', indicating it is the integration variable over the interface.

concepts_list.name: cal_T
concepts_list.description: The tensile pre-stress field, represented in script form to distinguish it from the spatial domain or other variables.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'T' (in script form) is used to denote the tensile pre-stress field, appearing in both the original PDE and the weak form, and is consistently used in the context of stress and gradients.

concepts_list.name: delta_Omega_N
concepts_list.description: The Neumann boundary of the domain Ω, where Neumann conditions are applied.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol '∂Ω_N' is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann conditions are imposed, and appears in the weak form as the domain of the boundary load 'g'.
